title: caladenia fluvialis
intro: Caladenia fluvialis, also known as the river spider orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. It is native to Western Australia, where it can be found growing in the wild, particularly in the southwestern region. The medicinal properties of Caladenia fluvialis include its potential use in treating various health issues, such as fever and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, and herbal preparations often involve steeping the plant in hot water to make a tea.
